title: Faithful C condenseStack single-pass port
description:
## Objective

Replace the 5-phase condenseStack with C's single-pass algorithm as originally planned in Step 5 of docs/ums-glr-timeout-analysis.md. The current implementation diverges significantly — it only applies decisive kills and ignores soft preferences (PreferLeft/PreferRight), which causes structural ambiguity failures (e.g., Go call/type_conv cluster, many of the 99 remaining corpus failures).

## Background

See docs/ums-plan-vs-implementation-audit.md for the full gap analysis. Key findings:
- Steps 1-4 of the UMS plan were done faithfully
- Step 5 uses a phased approach that omits soft preferences and swaps
- SwapVersions exists but is dead code
- Round-robin was added to compensate for missing swaps

## Tasks

1. Replace the 5-phase condenseStack with C's single-pass algorithm:
   - Single nested loop: for each version i, compare against all prior j < i
   - Handle TakeLeft/TakeRight (decisive kills) — already working
   - Handle PreferLeft/None → try merge (same state required)
   - Handle PreferRight → try merge, else SWAP
   - Remove same-position restriction on Phase 2
   - Remove vestigial Phase 3 (absolute cost threshold)
   - Hard cap: remove from end (relies on swap ordering)

2. Complete Step 6: Change Merge from halt-source to remove-source
   - Match C's ts_stack_merge which removes the source version
   - Update index management in condenseStack loop accordingly

3. Investigate swap regressions: Find what specific tests broke when swaps were
   tried previously. Determine if they were real regressions or tests passing for
   wrong reasons. Fix the root cause.

4. Test round-robin necessity: After swaps work, test whether round-robin
   findActiveVersion is still needed. If swaps properly reorder versions,
   starvation may be naturally prevented.

5. Write condenseStack unit tests (planned Step 7, not done)

6. Full corpus verification: target >= 1520 pass (no regressions from coder-2's
   current 1520/99/1619)

## Acceptance Criteria

- condenseStack matches C's ts_parser__condense_stack algorithm structure
- PreferLeft/PreferRight/None outcomes are handled (not just decisive kills)
- SwapVersions is called when needed (no longer dead code)
- Go call/type_conv cluster (bead nlb) resolves correctly
- No regressions from current 1520 pass rate
- condenseStack has unit tests
